description: Educational-proxy HKD liquidity-premium / funding-spread series, 2006-2025 monthly, expressed in absolute terms (0.006 = 60 bp). Interpreted as the NON-credit illiquidity-premium component of HKD corporate/covered asset spreads (EIOPA volatility-adjustment-style decomposition) and the funding-roll spread faced when liquidating or refinancing illiquid backing assets. Used to drive the Phase 21 Task 3 CIR++ liquidity-premium calibration (risks MR-011 / MR-012). EDUCATIONAL PROXY approximating the documented level and dispersion of published illiquidity-premium estimates; NOT a credentialled vendor extract. Replace with a credentialled covered-bond / swap-basis or VA-component series and re-run sign-off before any regulatory, pricing, or capital-adequacy use.

monthly_synthesis.method: A monthly square-root factor path x(t) is simulated from the documented target CIR parameters (target_mean_reversion kappa_l, long-run level b = target_long_run_premium - shift, target_premium_vol sigma_l) using full-truncation Euler x_{t+1} = x_t + kappa*(b - x_t)*dt + sigma*sqrt(max(x_t,0))*sqrt(dt)*Z, dt = 1/12, x_0 = initial_premium - shift. The realised premium = clip(x + shift, premium_floor, premium_ceiling). Fully deterministic given the seed. The homoscedastic CIR OLS calibrator recovers the long-run premium (sample-mean robust), the premium vol (residual-variance robust) and the mean-reversion speed (regression-slope, noisier) from this single path.

documented_sources_note: Published illiquidity-premium decompositions of investment-grade asset-swap spreads (EIOPA VA methodology; covered-bond vs swap basis; matching-adjustment literature) place the long-run illiquidity component for IG portfolios broadly in a 30-120 bp band, spiking to several hundred bp in funding stress (2008-09, Mar-2020) and compressing toward ~20-40 bp in benign markets. A CIR++ long-run level of 60 bp with sigma_l = 0.022 and kappa_l = 0.60 (half-life ~1.2 yr) reproduces that level, the slow stress-decay, and the non-negative skewed dispersion. The risk-neutral anchor of 75 bp reflects the documented premium that market-consistent (bond-implied) illiquidity components carry over real-world averages.

primary_sources: EIOPA volatility-adjustment / illiquidity-premium component methodology (educational proxy levels)
HKD investment-grade asset-swap and covered-bond vs swap basis history (educational proxy levels)
data_lineage.production_restriction: Proxy levels only. Replace with a credentialled liquidity-premium series (approved vendor, documented decomposition, with standard errors) and re-run sign-off before regulatory, pricing, or capital-adequacy use.

notes: 20 calendar years of monthly synthesis (240 monthly observations; the liquidity calibration needs >= 60). The premium is non-negative by construction (square-root diffusion + shift), matching the economic constraint that an illiquidity premium earned for holding illiquid assets cannot be persistently negative.
